NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF CLAIMS AND TO STAY ACTION;, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT KAUFF,MCGUIRE & MARGOLIS, LLP SUCCESSOR TO KAUFF, MCCLAIN & MCGUIRE, LLP A PARTNERSHIP HEARING SET FOR APR-24-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KAUFF, AND, MCGUIRE & MARGOLIS, LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF CLAIMS AND TO STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. NO BASIS TO RESCIND THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. NO FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ESTABLISH UNCONSCIONABILITY. THE ARBITRATION SHALL OCCUR IN NEW YORK. THE DISPUTE INVOLVES MORE THAN ONE PARTNER, AND THE ARBITRATION IS CURRENTLY PENDING IN NEW YORK. THIS CALIFORNIA ACTION IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION. THE COURT ADDS: ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: JOANN BRYCE, CSR 3321

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Of Claims And To Stay Action;
Off Calendar Apr-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KAUFF, and, MCGUIRE & MARGOLIS, LLP'S Motion to Compel Arbitration of Claims and to Stay Action. Appearances: Russell J. Brubaker, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415) 785-8737, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; J. Chris Sweeney, Esq. of Kauff McGire & Margolis LLP (310) 277-7550, appearing for Defendant KMM, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Compel Arbitration is granted. No basis to rescind the arbitration clause. No fraud in the inducement and plaintiff does not establish unconscionability. The arbitration shall occur in New York. The dispute involves more than one partner, and the arbitration is currently pending in New York. This California action is stayed pending arbitration. The Court adds: All objections are overruled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: JoAnn Bryce, CSR 3321
